title: ranunculus antygophyllus
intro: Ranunculus antygophyllus, also known as the lesser celandine, is a perennial plant with yellow flowers and heart-shaped leaves that grow in wetlands and along streams. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant has various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting against bacterial infections, and it has been used to treat conditions such as arthritis and gout. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams, and its dried leaves can be made into a powder for use in ointments and salves.
